title: Problem 1 (Erdős and Moser)
statement_text: What is the least integer $\operatorname{TT}(k)$ such that every oriented graph on $\operatorname{TT}(k)$ vertices has acyclic number at least $k$ ? What is $\vec{\alpha}(n)=\min\{\vec{\alpha}(D)\mid D\text{ is an oriented graph of order $n$}\}$ ?
context_text: Erdős and Moser (1964) initiated the directed Ramsey-type study of acyclic numbers in oriented graphs. They proved $2^{(k-1)/2} < \operatorname{TT}(k) \leq 2^{k-1}$, equivalently $\log_2 n+1 \leq \vec{\alpha}(n) \leq 2\log_2 n+2$, but only little progress has been made since then. The present paper studies the analogue restricted to triangle-free oriented graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves bounds on the triangle-free restriction $\vec{a}(n)$, showing $\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}-\varepsilon\right)\sqrt{n\log n}\leq\vec{a}(n)\leq\frac{107}{8}\sqrt{n}\log n$ for every $\varepsilon>0$ and $n$ large enough.
paper_title: Minimum acyclic number and maximum dichromatic number of oriented triangle-free graphs of a given order

title: Problem 2 (Neumann-Lara)
statement_text: What is $\vec{\chi}(n)=\max\{\vec{\chi}(D)\mid D\text{ is an oriented graph of order $n$}\}$?
context_text: Neumann-Lara studied the maximum dichromatic number of oriented graphs of a given order. Known bounds give $\frac{1}{2}\frac{n}{\log n+1}\leq\vec{\chi}(n)\leq(1+o(1))\frac{n}{\log n}$, a factor-of-2 gap. The present paper studies the analogue $\vec{t}(n)$ restricted to triangle-free oriented graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves bounds on the triangle-free restriction $\vec{t}(n)$, showing $\frac{8}{107}\frac{\sqrt{n}}{\log n}\leq\vec{t}(n)\leq\left(\sqrt{2}+\varepsilon\right)\sqrt{\frac{n}{\log n}}$ for every $\varepsilon>0$ and $n$ large enough, and determines $18\leq\vec{m}(3)\leq 25$ for the minimum order of a 3-dichromatic oriented triangle-free graph.
paper_title: Minimum acyclic number and maximum dichromatic number of oriented triangle-free graphs of a given order

title: Conjecture 1 (Harutyunyan and McDiarmid)
statement_text: For every oriented graph $H$, there is $\epsilon>0$ such that every $H$-free oriented graph $D$ of order $n$ satisfies $\vec{\alpha}(D)\geq n^{\epsilon}$ and $\vec{\chi}(D)\leq n^{1-\epsilon}$.
context_text: Harutyunyan and McDiarmid conjectured this polynomial separation in an unpublished manuscript. The conjecture is open even when $H$ is the directed 3-cycle, and it is a strengthening of the directed analogue of the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture. The present paper's main results address precisely the case $H=\overrightarrow{C_3}$.
paper_contribution: Verifies the conjecture for $H=\overrightarrow{C_3}$ (directed triangle): every triangle-free oriented graph on $n$ vertices satisfies $\vec{\alpha}(D)\geq\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}-o(1)\right)\sqrt{n\log n}=n^{1/2+o(1)}$ and $\vec{\chi}(D)\leq\left(\sqrt{2}+o(1)\right)\sqrt{n/\log n}=n^{1/2-o(1)}$.
notes: Source listed as unpublished in the bibliography.
paper_title: Minimum acyclic number and maximum dichromatic number of oriented triangle-free graphs of a given order

title: Conjecture 2 (Alon, Pach, and Solymosi)
statement_text: For every tournament $H$, there exists $\epsilon>0$ such that every $H$-free tournament $T$ of order $n$ satisfies $\vec{\alpha}(T)\geq n^{\epsilon}$.
context_text: Alon, Pach, and Solymosi (2001) posed this conjecture as a tournament analogue of the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture; it is equivalent to the celebrated Erdős–Hajnal conjecture and is a consequence of Conjecture 1. It is known to hold for a few types of tournaments but remains wide open in general.
paper_contribution: The paper's bounds on triangle-free oriented graphs provide supporting evidence for the conjecture in the directed setting, but do not directly prove any new case of this tournament-specific conjecture.
paper_title: Minimum acyclic number and maximum dichromatic number of oriented triangle-free graphs of a given order

title: Conjecture 3
context_text: The paper establishes $\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}-\varepsilon\right)\sqrt{n\log n}\leq\vec{a}(n)\leq\frac{107}{8}\sqrt{n}\log n$, a logarithmic gap between lower and upper bounds. The authors believe the upper bound can be sharpened to $C\sqrt{n\log n}$ by analysing a suitable orientation of the triangle-free process $G_{\triangle}$.
paper_title: Minimum acyclic number and maximum dichromatic number of oriented triangle-free graphs of a given order

title: Conjecture 4
context_text: Conjecture 4 follows from Conjecture 3, since $\vec{t}(n)\geq n/\vec{a}(n)$ and $\vec{t}(n)\leq t(n)$. The paper establishes $\frac{8}{107}\frac{\sqrt{n}}{\log n}\leq\vec{t}(n)\leq\left(\sqrt{2}+o(1)\right)\sqrt{\frac{n}{\log n}}$, leaving a logarithmic gap on the lower side.
notes: The LaTeX in the source appears to be missing parentheses around the $\Theta$ argument; reproduced verbatim as given.
paper_title: Minimum acyclic number and maximum dichromatic number of oriented triangle-free graphs of a given order
